title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
In a common room, Genavieve announces to a group of students that she is certain Nigel Chapman killed Silvia. She claims she overheard Nigel in Patricia's room explaining that his father was a murderer and why he changed his name. Other students, including Mr. Chandra Lal, Gene, and Ahmed Ali, speculate on Nigel's character and motives, while Leonard Betson reacts angrily to their theories.

Meanwhile, at a police station, Nigel provides a statement to Inspector Sharp. Nigel explains that Patricia Lane is in a state of mental chaos and cannot remember when she last saw a morphine bottle. During their conversation, a constable receives a call from Patricia. She sounds agitated and claims to know who took the bottle from her handkerchief drawer, but the line goes dead before she can name the person.

Inspector Sharp and Nigel travel to 26 Walnut Street. Upon entering Patricia's room, they discover Patricia Ryan dead on the floor. Sharp determines she was killed by a blow to the back of the head with a marble paperweight wrapped in a wool sock. Nigel identifies the paperweight as a "Luzern Lion" and becomes distraught, noting the sock belonged to him.

Sharp begins questioning Jeremiah to establish a timeline. Jeremiah states that between 6:00 and 6:35, Nigel, Mrs. Habberd, and Valerie Hobhouse were absent. He notes that Nigel left at 6:00 looking agitated and Valerie left at 6:00 dressed semi-formally. Miss Sally returned after 6:00 to post a letter. Sharp notes that Patricia's call occurred at 6:08, leaving Nigel, Valerie, and Mrs. Habberd as the primary individuals who cannot be accounted for.
